findings.title: Excessive Comment Ratio in contextRanking.ts (39%)
findings.description:
contextRanking.ts has 171 comment lines out of 440 total lines (39% comment ratio, just below the 40% threshold). Every public function carries a multi-line JSDoc block with @param and @returns tags that restate what the TypeScript signature already encodes. For example:

--- code begin (reference only) ---
/**
 * Score a file based on its path depth
 *
 * Shallower files get higher scores (closer to repository root).
 * Normalized to 0-1 range.
 *
 * @param relativePath - Path relative to repository root
 * @returns Score between 0 and 1
 */
export function scoreByPathDepth(relativePath: string): number {
--- code end ---

The function name, parameter type, and return type already communicate everything the JSDoc repeats. Inline comments like `// Count path segments (slashes)` and `// Normalize: depth 0 = 1.0, depth 5+ = 0.0` describe code that is self-evident. The file also uses 8 section-banner dividers (`// ===...===`) to separate small blocks of 2-4 functions, adding structural noise without value.
findings.suggested_remediation: Remove @param and @returns JSDoc tags where the TypeScript signature is self-documenting. Collapse the 8 section-banner dividers into a single file-level comment. Retain only comments that explain non-obvious scoring decisions (e.g., why maxDepth=5 or maxAgeDays=180).

findings.title: Excessive Section-Banner Dividers Across Queue Module (20 banners per file)
findings.description:
The queue subsystem files each contain 14-20 `// ===...===` section-banner dividers. Files affected include queueTypes.ts (20), queueMemoryIndex.ts (20), queueOperationsLog.ts (14), queueStore.ts, queueSnapshotManager.ts, queueCompactionEngine.ts, and queueTaskManager.ts. These banners divide tiny blocks of 1-3 declarations under section headings like `// --- Types ---`, `// --- Queue Writing ---`, `// --- Queue Reading ---`. This is a classic AI-generated code pattern where every logical grouping is given a heavyweight visual separator regardless of actual module size.

--- code begin (reference only) ---
// --- Queue Writing ---

/**
 * Append tasks to queue.
 * Uses V2 WAL for atomic task creation with batch support.
 */
export async function appendToQueue(...
--- code end ---

The queue module is split across ~10 files, so these internal dividers serve no navigation purpose.
findings.suggested_remediation: Remove all `// --- Section ---` and `// ===...===` dividers inside queue module files. Module-level organization already provided by the separate file-per-concern structure. Retain at most one file-header comment block per file.

findings.title: Pervasive Section-Banner Dividers in Core Config and Telemetry Files
findings.suggested_remediation: Establish a linting rule or team convention: no section-banner dividers inside files. Navigation belongs to the file system, not ASCII art. Do a single-pass removal across all 56 affected files.

findings.title: Boilerplate Factory Functions on Every Adapter and Service
findings.suggested_remediation: Delete no-op factory functions and call constructors directly at call sites. If dependency injection or future factory logic is genuinely anticipated, keep a single factory with a comment explaining why. Do not delete if tests mock these factories by name.

findings.title: Symmetric Before/After Logger Calls on Every Adapter Method
findings.suggested_remediation: Remove before/after debug logs from adapter methods. Keep only error-path logs. The HttpClient already records request/response telemetry. If operation-level tracing is needed, use the existing TraceManager spans rather than log statements.

findings.title: Redundant Step-Numbering Comments in CLI Commands and Workflows
findings.suggested_remediation: Remove all `// Step N:` comments. If execution order is non-obvious, extract the steps into a named array or state machine rather than embedding ordinal comments.

findings.title: Excessive JSDoc on safeJson.ts Utility Functions (56% comment ratio)
findings.suggested_remediation: Remove @example blocks and reduce @param/@returns to a single-line JSDoc summary for each function. The TypeScript signature is the documentation. Retain only the file-level comment explaining the purpose of the module.

findings.title: Verbose JSDoc Blocks on Interface Fields in writeActionQueueTypes.ts (48% comment ratio)
findings.suggested_remediation: Remove per-field JSDoc from interface definitions where the field name and type are self-explanatory. Retain JSDoc only for fields with non-obvious constraints, valid ranges, or unusual semantics (e.g., `/** @deprecated use X instead */`).

findings.title: Verbose JSDoc on Interface Fields in httpTypes.ts and branchProtection.ts
findings.suggested_remediation: Remove per-field JSDoc where field name plus type is sufficient. Apply a team convention: JSDoc on interface fields only when clarifying units (e.g., milliseconds vs seconds), valid value constraints, or non-obvious semantics.

findings.title: Redundant 'Ensure directory exists' Inline Comments Before fs.mkdir
findings.suggested_remediation: Delete all `// Ensure X directory exists` comments. The code is self-documenting.

findings.title: Redundant 'Clean up temp file on error' Comments in Atomic Write Pattern
findings.suggested_remediation: Remove inline comments from the cleanup catch block. Consider extracting `atomicWriteFile(path, content)` as a shared utility to eliminate the 7-site duplication of the identical 10-line pattern.

findings.title: Duplicated Atomic Write Pattern Across 7+ Files
findings.suggested_remediation: Extract a shared `atomicWriteFile(filePath: string, content: string): Promise<void>` utility in src/utils/ and replace all 7+ sites. Reduces ~70 lines of duplicated boilerplate to a single tested utility.

findings.title: Obvious Inline Comments in scoreByPathDepth and scoreBySize
findings.suggested_remediation: Remove comments that describe the adjacent line of code verbatim. Retain only comments that explain the rationale for a constant value or non-obvious algorithmic choice.

findings.title: Verbose JSDoc on Trivially-Obvious @param Blocks in runDirectoryManager.ts
findings.description:
runDirectoryManager.ts (1,144 lines, 29% comment ratio, 40 inline JSDoc annotations) has 47 `@param` tags across its exported functions. The majority describe parameters whose names are entirely self-explanatory:

--- code begin (reference only) ---
/**
 * Get the absolute path to a run directory
 *
 * @param baseDir - Base pipeline directory (.codepipe/runs)
 * @param featureId - Feature identifier
 * @returns Absolute path to run directory
 * @throws Error if featureId contains path traversal sequences
 */
export function getRunDirectoryPath(baseDir: string, featureId: string): string {

/**
 * Get path to a subdirectory within a run directory
 *
 * @param runDir - Run directory path
 * @param subdir - Subdirectory name
 * @returns Absolute path to subdirectory
 */
export function getSubdirectoryPath(
--- code end ---

Parameters `baseDir`, `runDir`, `featureId`, `step`, `message`, `hook` need no @param documentation. The @throws tag on getRunDirectoryPath is useful and should be retained. This pattern repeats across ~30 functions.
findings.suggested_remediation: Remove @param and @returns JSDoc tags where the TypeScript signature is complete. Retain @throws, @deprecated, and comments explaining non-obvious constraints. Target: eliminate ~100 lines of redundant JSDoc.

findings.title: Implements/FR Reference Comments in File Headers With No Live Link
findings.description:
12+ files open with a JSDoc block citing internal document references like `FR-7`, `FR-15`, `IR-5`, `ADR-6`, `Section 2.1`, `Task I4.T5`:

--- code begin (reference only) ---
/**
 * Branch Protection Reporter
 * ...
 * Implements:
 * - FR-15: Status checks mandate
 * - Section 2.1: Deployment trigger module responsibilities
 * - Task I4.T5: Branch protection intelligence integration
 */

/**
 * GitHub Adapter
 * ...
 * Implements:
 * - Section 2.1: Key Components - GitHub Adapter
 * - IR-1..IR-7: Integration requirements
 * - FR-15: PR automation
 */
--- code end ---

These references point to planning documents that are not in the repository (no docs/ folder contains FR-15.md or similar). The references cannot be navigated, verified, or updated. This is a common AI-generation artifact where requirement traceability comments are added by default but reference documents that may no longer match the implementation.
findings.suggested_remediation: Either link these references to navigable documents (e.g., docs/requirements/FR-15.md) or remove the Implements section from file headers. Dead references are worse than no references.

findings.title: Boilerplate Error Class Constructors with Identical Structure
findings.suggested_remediation: Consider making AdapterError accept a `name` parameter, allowing: `new AdapterError(message, type, statusCode, requestId, operation, 'GitHubAdapterError')`. This eliminates 3 subclasses. Alternatively keep subclasses but document why they exist (e.g., `instanceof` checks in tests).

findings.title: Narrating Comments in approvalRegistry.ts grantApproval Function
findings.suggested_remediation: Remove all narrating comments in grantApproval, denyApproval, and requestApproval except the deadlock avoidance note. The function names and variable names are self-documenting.

findings.title: Over-Specified JSDoc on Trivial Private Helper Methods in LinearAdapter
findings.suggested_remediation: Remove JSDoc from private methods in LinearAdapter (and similarly in GitHubAdapter and BranchProtectionAdapter). Reserve JSDoc for public API surface only.

findings.title: Redundant Inline Comment on loadReport ENOENT Branch
findings.suggested_remediation: Remove inline comments on ENOENT catch branches. The error code is universally understood.

findings.title: JSDoc @example Blocks on safeJsonParseWithResult and safeJsonParseValidated
findings.suggested_remediation: Remove @example blocks from safeJsonParseWithResult and safeJsonParseValidated. Keep the single-sentence summary comment. This reduces the file by ~30 lines.
